title: Emergency Guide: Puppy Stuck in Birth Canal for Over 30 Minutes
meta_desc: A puppy visible in the birth canal for 30+ minutes is a veterinary emergency. Learn what dystocia means for your dog and why immediate help is vital.
content_html:
What is Dystocia?

When a puppy is visible at the vulva but fails to be delivered within 15 to 30 minutes, it is a medical condition known as dystocia, or difficult labor. This occurs when the puppy is physically stuck due to its size or position, or when the mother's uterine muscles are too exhausted to complete the delivery process.

Urgency Level: High Emergency

This is a high-urgency emergency. If a puppy has been visible for 30 minutes without progress, the puppy's oxygen supply is likely compromised because the placenta may have detached. Without immediate veterinary intervention, the puppy is at extreme risk of stillbirth, and the mother faces risks of infection, uterine rupture, or life-threatening exhaustion.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

If you are in contact with an emergency vet, sending a clear photo of the puppy's position can be life-saving. A veterinarian can use the image to determine if the puppy is breech, if the amniotic sac is intact, or if the mother's tissues appear healthy. This visual information allows the triage team to provide specific instructions on whether a gentle home assist is safe or if you must depart for the clinic immediately.

faqs.question: Can I pull the puppy out myself?
faqs.answer: You should never pull a puppy forcefully. If a veterinarian advises it, you may use a clean towel to gently provide traction only during a contraction, but improper technique can cause fatal internal damage to both the mother and the pup.

faqs.question: What if the mother is not straining?
faqs.answer: If the puppy is visible but the mother has stopped contracting, this is a sign of uterine inertia. This requires immediate medical intervention, as the mother can no longer move the puppy on her own.

faqs.question: How long is too long between puppies?
faqs.answer: While up to two hours between puppies can be normal if the mother is resting comfortably, any puppy partially visible for more than 15-30 minutes is an immediate crisis.
